sections.paragraph:
κελεύω (Hom.+) impf. ἐκέλευον; fut. κελεύσω (TestAbr B); 1 aor. ἐκέλευσα; plpf. 3 sg. ἐκεκελεύκει (Just., D. 103, 3). Pass.: aor. ἐκελεύσθην (TestAbr A, Just.); ptc. pl. κελευσθέντες 4 Macc 9:11; pf. pass. 1 pl. κεκελεύσμεθα (Just., D. 48, 4) to give a command, ordinarily of an official nature, command, order, urge foll. by the aor. inf. (B-D-F §338, 2; Rob. 857), which indicates the action to be carried out; the pers. who receives the order is in the acc. (SibOr 3, 298; w. acc. and ἵνα TestAbr B 11 p. 116, 6 [Stone p. 80]) not in Mk, J, Paul, B, IPol. κέλευσόν με ἐλθεῖν πρός σε command me to come to you Mt 14:28, cp. vs. 19; Ac 4:15; 22:30; 23:10; 24:8 v.l.; 1 Cl 43:2. This constr. can also be understood simply as acc. w. inf. as such, as plainly Ac 8:38; MPol 16:1.—The constr. in which κ. is followed by the acc. and the pass. inf., indicating that something is to happen to someone or something without giving the person who is to carry out the command, is more in accord w. Lat. usage than w. older Gk. (TestJob 5:3 ἀσφαλισθῆναι τὰς θύρας; s. B-D-F §5, 3b; 392, 4; Rob. 111) ἐκέλευσεν αὐτὸν ὁ κύριος πραθῆναι Mt 18:25. Cp. 27:64; Lk 18:40; Ac 12:19; 25:6, 17; so esp. Hs 9, 8, 5 (λίθους) ἐκέλευσεν εἰς τὴν οἰκοδομὴν τεθῆναι al.; ἐκέλευσεν αὐτῷ (against Paul) ἀφεθῆναι λέοντα AcPl Ha 4, 19; λυθῆναι τοὺς δεσμίους 11, 9.—W. inf. alone, so that everything else must be supplied fr. context Mt 8:18; 14:9; 27:58; Ac 5:34; 21:33; MPol 7:2.—The pres. inf. occurs more rarely (B-D-F §338, 2): w. acc. (X., An. 2, 1, 8; Chariton 7, 6, 2; TestJob 9:6; TestJos 13:9) Ac 21:34; 22:24; 23:3, 35; 25:21; 27:43; 1 Cl 33:3; 40:1f; Hs 8, 1, 6f; 9, 3, 1 and 3f. Without acc. (X., Cyr. 2, 2, 2; Appian, Liby. 55 §241; Herodian 2, 3, 3) Ac 16:22; Dg 7:2.—Abs. κελεύσαντος τοῦ Φήστου at Festus’ command Ac 25:23 (cp. Michel 594, 53 [279 b.c.] ἐπιμελητῶν κελευόντων; Jos., Ant. 11, 78 Δαρείου κελεύσαντος). θελήματι τοῦ κελεύοντος λόγου Dg 11:8.—W. dat. of pers. and inf. (Longus 3, 8, 2; Polyaenus 3, 10, 11; Tob 8:18 BA; ApcMos 27:29 [aor.].—Thu. 1, 44, 1; Diod S 19, 17, 3; Herm. Wr. 1, 29; 2 Macc 5:12; EpArist 184; TestJob 9:8; Jos., Ant. 20, 132 [pres.]; w. dat. and ἵνα TestJob 39:8) Mt 15:35 v.l. for παραγγείλας (κ. is also toned down to urgently request, invite: Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 4 p. 332, 9ff Jac.; Epict., Fgm. 17 in an invitation; Arrian, Anab. 2, 6, 1 his friends do this to Alex. the Great; 3, 9, 3; 7, 2, 1; 7, 8, 3) AcPl Ha 4, 10.—Pass. in personal construction (TestAbr B 7 p. 112, 12 [Stone p. 72]) καθὼς ἐκελεύσθησαν Hs 9, 4, 1.—B. 1337. DELG. M-M. TW. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

aor. aor. = aorist

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pl. = plural
  2. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

foll. foll. = followed, following

inf. inf. = infinitive

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

pers. pers. = person(s)

acc. acc. = accusative

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

constr. constr. = construction

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Gk. Gk. = Greek

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

esp. esp. = especially

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

  1. fr. = from

pres. pres. = present

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

dat. dat. = dative

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 538
